reforms.description: The Costa Rican government is establishing the Agencia Costarricense de Fomento Productivo, Innovación y Valor Agregado (FOMPRODUCE) to centralize funds and functions currently dispersed across several agencies, facilitating business establishment and promoting innovation. This initiative includes better enforcement of intellectual property rights and a shift in public R&D spending towards tertiary education institutions to strengthen connections with local innovative enterprises and improve job prospects for technical graduates. Additionally, policies have been adopted to promote businesses' innovation and linkages between foreign and domestic companies, including funding programmes like PROPYME and CRP, which have shown positive impacts on beneficiaries' performance.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of FOMPRODUCE and the associated policies are expected to enhance innovation and business performance, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The FOMPRODUCE agency has been established and is operational, coordinating various innovation programs effectively.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of a centralized agency for innovation and the significant policy shifts represent a structural change in Costa Rica's innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The government is seeking to deepen Costa Rica’s participation in the global value-added chains of four sectors... by attracting foreign companies operating in these fields.

reforms.description: Costa Rica aims to increase the share of public R&D spending directed towards higher education institutions to strengthen connections with local innovative enterprises and improve job prospects for technical graduates.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public R&D funding for higher education institutions is expected to enhance innovation capacity and job prospects, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has publicly stated its intention to increase R&D funding for higher education institutions.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is more focused and does not represent a system-wide change.
reforms.source_quote: Increase the share of public R&D spending channeled to higher education institutions to strengthen links with local innovative enterprises and enhance job prospects for technical graduates.
